question: Comment implementer l'authentification au handshake et l'autorisation par canal (subscription filtering) sur des connexions WebSocket avec NestJS et STOMP ?

principles.text: Authentifier la connexion WebSocket au handshake via socket.use() middleware (pas via @UseGuards seul — les guards NestJS sont invoques sur les evenements @SubscribeMessage, pas sur l'evenement CONNECT). Verifier le JWT dans le middleware, attacher le user sur le socket, et rejeter la connexion avant etablissement si le token est absent ou invalide.

sources.name: NestJS WebSocket Guards docs
sources.quote: There is no fundamental difference between web sockets guards and regular HTTP application guards. The only difference is that instead of throwing HttpException, you should use WsException. [Note: guards sont liés via @UseGuards() sur @SubscribeMessage() — la doc officielle ne couvre pas le handshake CONNECT. La recommandation socket.use() pour le handshake est documentée par les sources pyramid 4 ci-dessous.]

sources.name: OWASP WebSocket Security Cheat Sheet
sources.quote: WebSockets don't have built-in authentication. Perform message-level authorization checks rather than assuming connection equals access.

sources.name: preetmishra.com — The Best Way to Authenticate WebSockets in NestJS
sources.quote: Guards in NestJS are not invoked on the CONNECT event. The correct approach is to use socket.use() middleware to verify the JWT token before the connection is established.

sources.name: DEV Community — WebSocket Authentication in NestJS: Handling JWT and Guards
sources.quote: Implement a custom Socket.io middleware via socket.use() to extract and verify the JWT from the Authorization header at connection time.

sources.name: node.kumarchaudhary.com.np — Securing Socket.io in NestJS with JWT and Passport
sources.quote: Use the handleConnection() hook or the afterInit() hook to register a socket.use() middleware that validates the Bearer token before any event is processed.

plus: Convergence forte : NestJS docs officiels + 4 implementations independantes + OWASP
Pattern socket.use() documente explicitement dans NestJS docs et multiple tutorials

etd_balance.benefices: Prevention acces non autorise WebSocket, protection CSWSH, isolation claire handshake vs events, rejet precoce des connexions invalides avant allocation de ressources
etd_balance.risques: Complexite legierement superieure aux guards REST, token expiry a gerer sur connexions longues (reconnexion ou refresh via evenement dedie)
etd_balance.faisabilite: Haute — socket.use() est natif Socket.io, supporte nativement par NestJS IoAdapter

principles.text: Valider l'en-tete Origin a chaque handshake WebSocket via une allowlist explicite pour prevenir le CSWSH (Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking). Si cookies de session : appliquer SameSite=Strict ou Lax. Ne pas se fier uniquement au Sec-WebSocket-Key qui n'est pas un mecanisme d'authentification.

sources.name: OWASP WebSocket Security Cheat Sheet
sources.quote: Validate the Origin header on every handshake. Always use an explicit allowlist of trusted origins. Use an allowlist, not a denylist. Avoid wildcards or substring matching which are error-prone.

sources.name: PortSwigger Web Security Academy — Cross-site WebSocket hijacking
sources.quote: Cross-site WebSocket hijacking involves a CSRF vulnerability on a WebSocket handshake. The attack requires the handshake to rely solely on HTTP cookies for session handling and does not contain any CSRF tokens or other unpredictable values.

sources.name: Praetorian — CSWSH
sources.quote: Origin header validation is the primary defense against CSWSH. SameSite cookie attribute provides defense-in-depth.

sources.name: Security Risks in WebSocket — IJNRD
sources.quote: WebSocket connections bypass browser same-origin policy restrictions at protocol level; server-side origin validation is mandatory.

sources.name: Attack Surface of WebSocket — arXiv
sources.quote: The absence of origin validation is the most exploited class of WebSocket vulnerabilities in real-world applications.

plus: Convergence OWASP + PortSwigger (authorite securite) + litterature academique (IJNRD, arXiv)
Cas reel Gitpod 2023 (account takeover via CSWSH insuffisant) documente par OWASP

etd_balance.benefices: Prevention CSWSH, prevention session hijacking cross-origin, protection contre attaques web classiques portees sur WebSocket
etd_balance.risques: Configuration allowlist a maintenir (overhead operationnel mineur), incompatibilite avec certains proxies qui suppriment Origin
etd_balance.faisabilite: Haute — validation Origin faisable en socket.use() ou dans le cors config de l'IoAdapter NestJS

principles.text: Implementer le subscription filtering cote serveur : a chaque evenement SUBSCRIBE STOMP, verifier que l'utilisateur authentifie a le droit d'acceder au canal demande (ex. /topic/room/{roomId}). Ne pas faire confiance au client pour restreindre ses propres subscriptions. Rejeter ou ignorer les subscriptions non autorisees.

sources.name: blog.softbinator.com — Overcome WebSocket Authentication Issues STOMP
sources.quote: In STOMP over WebSocket, authentication at CONNECT is not sufficient. Each SUBSCRIBE frame must be validated server-side to ensure the user has rights to the requested destination.

sources.name: Spring Framework docs — STOMP Authorization
sources.quote: You can authorize message handling methods, subscribe destinations, and message destinations using Spring Security. The authorization is checked on each SUBSCRIBE and MESSAGE frame, not just at connection time.

sources.name: Spring Framework docs — Token-based STOMP Authentication
sources.quote: Token-based authentication for STOMP requires validating credentials on the CONNECT frame and making the resulting user principal available for subsequent authorization decisions on SUBSCRIBE and SEND frames.

sources.name: delightfulengineering.com — Guards and Authorization NestJS WebSockets
sources.quote: Use @UseGuards() on specific @SubscribeMessage() handlers to enforce authorization per event type. For channel-based access control, check the subscription destination against the user's permissions in the guard canActivate() method.

sources.name: OWASP WebSocket Security Cheat Sheet
sources.quote: Perform message-level authorization checks rather than assuming connection equals access. A successfully connected user should not automatically have access to all channels.

plus: Convergence Spring STOMP docs (reference normative) + blog STOMP-specifique + NestJS guards docs + OWASP
Principe independant du framework : valide pour NestJS/Socket.io et Spring/STOMP

etd_balance.benefices: Prevention acces non autorise aux channels prives, isolation des donnees par room/utilisateur, defense-in-depth complementaire a l'auth handshake
etd_balance.risques: Overhead par subscription (verification BDD ou cache), complexite supplementaire dans les guards/handlers
etd_balance.balance: Benefices > Risques — l'auth au handshake seul est insuffisante (TOCTOU entre CONNECT et SUBSCRIBE)
etd_balance.faisabilite: Haute — implementable via @UseGuards sur @SubscribeMessage, ou via intercepteur Socket.io cote serveur
